name: default-list-token
description: Buyer save-to-list (src/lib/lists + /api/me/lists): the save button PUTs /api/me/lists/default/items/[listingId]; repo.resolveListForWrite maps the reserved 'default' token to getOrCreateDefaultList (lazy wishlist creation, race-safe via onConflictDoNothing on the (user_id,name) unique index) so the client never pre-resolves the wishlist id. Every list route/page gates on requireActor()/getActor() (is-authenticated), never a role check (spec 3.1); a non-owned list resolves 404 not 403.

title: Custom saved lists are hard-deleted, not soft-deleted
rationale: The lists(user_id,name) unique index ignores deleted_at, so a soft-deleted 'Beach plots' would block re-creating a list with that name. R-12 soft-delete exists for listing/lead resolvability (sold/removed listings still render, greyed out), not a buyer's own folders — so deleteList issues a real DELETE and list_items cascade via FK. price_at_save is snapshotted on add so the account page can flag that the asking price moved since saving.
